name: True Crime
description: Famous criminals, infamous cases, and the names that shaped crime history.

questions.q: What nickname was given to the unidentified Whitechapel serial killer of 1888 London?

source.notes: Five 'canonical' victims in the Whitechapel district.

questions.q: Which 1929 mass shooting on Valentine's Day in Chicago is associated with Al Capone?

source.notes: Feb 14, 1929 — 7 men killed in a Chicago garage.

questions.q: Which mob boss famously got jailed not for racketeering — but for tax evasion?

source.notes: Sentenced to 11 years in 1931.

questions.q: Which Australian outlaw famously wore homemade metal armor in his final shootout?

source.notes: Killed at Glenrowan, 1880.

questions.q: Who shot John Lennon outside the Dakota apartment building in NYC in 1980?

source.notes: December 8, 1980. Still in prison.

questions.q: Which U.S. president did John Hinckley Jr. attempt to assassinate in 1981?

source.notes: March 30, 1981 outside the Washington Hilton. Hinckley wanted to impress actress Jodie Foster.

questions.q: Who assassinated President Abraham Lincoln in 1865?

source.notes: April 14, 1865 at Ford's Theatre.

questions.q: Who was charged with assassinating President JFK on November 22, 1963?

source.notes: Killed by Jack Ruby two days later before standing trial.

questions.q: Which Colombian drug lord ran the Medellín Cartel and was killed in 1993?

source.notes: Subject of Netflix's 'Narcos.'

questions.q: Which Mexican drug lord — leader of the Sinaloa Cartel — was extradited to the U.S. in 2017?

source.notes: Sentenced to life in 2019.

questions.q: Who is the international police organization headquartered in Lyon, France?

source.notes: International Criminal Police Organization. 195 member countries.

questions.q: Who founded the FBI and led it 1924-1972?

source.notes: Director for 48 years.

questions.q: Which 1969 'Helter Skelter' murders involved actress Sharon Tate's killing?

source.notes: August 9, 1969. Tate was 8 months pregnant.

questions.q: Which 1995 trial — for the murder of Nicole Brown Simpson — is often called the 'trial of the century'?

source.notes: Acquitted October 3, 1995. Found liable in civil court 1997.

questions.q: Which UK doctor was convicted of killing 15 patients (suspected of ~250)?

source.notes: Convicted 2000; killed himself in prison 2004.

questions.q: Which serial killer murdered 17 men/boys in Milwaukee 1978-1991?

source.notes: Killed in prison by another inmate, 1994.

questions.q: Which Wichita-area killer self-named 'BTK' (Bind, Torture, Kill) was caught in 2005?

source.notes: 10 victims 1974-1991. Caught after sending taunting letters again in 2004.

questions.q: Which 1992 Pacific Northwest serial killer was nicknamed 'Green River Killer'?

source.notes: Convicted of 49 murders in 2003; suspected of 70+.

questions.q: Which 2009 Johnny Depp film depicts the famous Public Enemy bank robber John Dillinger?

source.notes: Directed by Michael Mann.

questions.q: Which 1932 kidnapping case shocked America with the abduction of an aviator's son?

source.notes: 20-month-old Charles Lindbergh Jr. kidnapped from his crib. Bruno Hauptmann executed in 1936.
